agendas.title: Ask Me Anything with Taylor's Mereka Makerspace: Gearing up for Tomorrow - My Social Enterprise Journey
agendas.description: Juliana invites you to have an intimate conversation on her journey in being a social entrepreneur. She will give insights on how she started the numerous social enterprises Biji-biji Initiative, Me.Reka, Taboo Enterprise and many others. Juliana will also talk about the struggles she face while growing and leading a team of whilst giving back to the community.

agendas.title: Do-It-Yourself @ Home: Build Your Remote Control Car
agendas.description: Want to build your very own wooden remote control car? Join this workshop to learn the fundamentals of mechanical engineering and electronics in assembling this zippy car. You'll also get hands-on experience in using a laser cutter to make the body of the car.

agendas.title: Eat or Spill It with Taylor's Christian Fellowship & Taylor's Rotaract Club
agendas.description: Watch both these clubs take truth-provoking questions to a whole new level! Will they spill the truth or suffer the consequences? Tune in to our IG Live to find out!

agendas.title: The Blind Snack Test with Taylor's International Student Society x Taylor's Bangladesh Society x Taylor's African Society & Komando45
agendas.description: Watch which clubs could guess these delightful international treats right especially when the only thing that could help them is their taste buds! Tune in to our IG Live to find out!

agendas.title: BizPod Workshop: Do This Before Your Start-up
agendas.description:
Build the foundation to a potentially awesome business in just 6 easy steps!

  1. No Problem, No Idea
  2. The Art of Ideation
  3. So Many Ideas, So Little Time
  4. Draw It If You Can, Build It If You Must 
  5. Test, Test, Test
  6. Making It Rain

allAgendas.title: Ask Me Anything with Taylor's Mereka Makerspace: Gearing up for Tomorrow - My Social Enterprise Journey

allAgendas.title: The Blind Snack Test with Taylor's International Student Society x Taylor's Bangladesh Society x Taylor's African Society & Komando45
allAgendas.description: Watch which clubs could guess these delightful international treats right especially when the only thing that could help them is their taste buds! Tune in to our IG Live to find out!

agendas.title: Ask Me Anything with Taylor's Mereka Makerspace: Gearing up for Tomorrow - Makerspaces
agendas.description: Johnson Lam is a community builder who founded KakiDIY. Focuses on DIY education mainly on life-skills and STE(A)M; inspiring makers to be solving problems using resources around us. Johnson invites you to an intimate conversation on how Makerspaces and the Maker spirit is vital for everyone’s own educational journey and personal development. He will also talk about his experience on how develop his makerspace and how the makers spirit can lead to a better sustainable technologically advanced society.

agendas.title: Do-It-Yourself @ Home: Build Your Bluetooth Speaker
agendas.description: Ever wonder how you can build a wooden bluetooth speaker? Through this workshop, learn the fundamentals of fabrication and electronics in assembling your own speakers to play tunes at home. Also, you'll get hands-on experience in using a laser cutter to make the body of the speakers with your own customised details.

agendas.title: Mirror These Moves with Taylor's Karate Club and Taylor's Taekwondo Club
agendas.description: Can these martial art scenes we see in movies be mirrored in real life? Watch these clubs bring the closest demonstration of these scenes to reality with a battle of who can mirror the moves the best. Tune in to our IG Live to find out!

agendas.title: Table Topics™️ with Taylor's Toastmasters Club and Taylor's Lakeside Model United Nations
agendas.description: Watch both these clubs battle out each other in a round of interesting table topics! Who had the best speech? That's for you to decide! Tune in to our IG Live to find out!

agendas.title: Microcred Tasters: Competitive Strategic Analysis
agendas.description: Explore strategic leadership tools for managing and monitoring internal and external events to provide timely execution in order to maintain a competitive advantage.

agendas.title: BizPod Workshop: Build a Tech Prototype in 24 Hours
agendas.description:
This workshop introduces you to a “no-code” way of prototyping a minimum viable product (MVP) in less than 24 hours. We’re going to:

  1. Introduce case studies of “no-code” products and companies, and examine the philosophy and tools behind them
  2. Understand how to prototype an MVP, including what tools to use and steps to take 
  3. Live-demo building a feature for an sample student project

agendas.title: Launch of Taylor's ADP 25th Anniversary Virtual Celebration
agendas.description: Taylor’s ADP 25th Anniversary event aims to celebrate the excellence instilled in the American Degree Transfer Program. Come and enjoy this fun-filled virtual Event Launch Ceremony with us, marking the start of our year-long virtual celebration activities, including the Virtual Donation Drive, American Festival, Talent Competition and many more.

agendas.title: Microcred Tasters: Be a Future Ready Accountant
agendas.description: Join the class to understand about the evolution of responsibilities for accountancy professionals and learn about the mindset to fit into the future needs and business environment.

agendas.title: Ask Me Anything with Taylor's Mereka Makerspace: Gearing up for Tomorrow - Serving the Underserved
agendas.description: Dr Hartini is a tireless advocate for children in Malaysia and founded the Yayasan Chow Kit that caters for the needs of children and teenagers in Chow Kit. The foundation runs two-drop in centres and one safe home for children who are in need of temporary guardianship. Dr Hartini invites you to take part in an intimate conversation on how students, parents and the community can help the underserved in our society for them to have a better future ahead of them. She will talk about the challenges she faced in her work and also the stories of those who were able to make a better life for themselves due to the help of the community.

agendas.title: Do-It-Yourself @ Home: Build Your WiFi RGB LED Lamp
agendas.description: Wondering how you can build your own WiFi RGB LED Lamp? Join this interesting workshop to learn how to make your own WiFi-controlled lamp, while learning the basics of electronic assembly and app development. Also, get hands-on experience in using a laser cutter to make the body of the lamp with your own customised details.

agendas.title: Can You Guess Right with Taylor's Flix Society and Taylor's ETC Magazine
agendas.description: Can they guess the movie or can they guess the article? The only thing we can guess for sure is that things are about to get competitive between these two clubs! Tune in to our IG Live to find out!

agendas.title: Never Have We Ever with Taylor's MSA and Taylor's Chinese Society
agendas.description: Never have we ever looked forward to juicy moments like this *drinks water*. Which club has the most never's? Tune in to our IG Live to find out!

agendas.title: Microcred Tasters: Dynamic Pitching For StartUps
agendas.description: This course allows entrepreneurs to learn the art of dynamic pitching for their startups or business venture. Participants will learn the key tricks in preparing a pitching deck and the methods to deliver the pitch.

agendas.title: BizPod Workshop: The Business of E-commerce
agendas.description:
Aspire to set up your own online store? Learn the basics here!

  1. E-commerce ecosystem and online business
  2. The functions of different e-commerce platforms and marketplaces from B2B2C/C2C
  3. International trade and export e-commerce
  4. What do online customers/buyers want?
  5. Measuring the success of an e-commerce store

allAgendas.title: Microcred Tasters: Be a Future Ready Accountant
allAgendas.description: Join the class to understand about the evolution of responsibilities for accountancy professionals and learn about the mindset to fit into the future needs and business environment.

allAgendas.description: Never have we ever looked forward to juicy moments like this *drinks water*. Which club has the most never's? Tune in to our IG Live to find out!

agendas.title: Microcred Tasters: Menu That Brings Profit: Menu Design & Development
agendas.description: Want to learn the psychology and practical theories behind building usable and profitable menus for food business establishments? Understand how different strategic objectives for different operations affect menu design, and how profitability should be designed into item and category layouts. Full, interactive case studies and discussion questions are provided, which will have you designing new menus and editing existing menus based on industry best practices and latest academic findings.

agendas.title: Microcred Tasters: Big Data Technologies
agendas.description: Get the knack of data pre-processing fundamentals. Sample this Microcred that touches on organisational data preparation, exploratory data analytics, data warehouse, simple linear regression, multiple linear regression, support vector regression, decision tree regression and evaluation of regression models performance.

agendas.title: Ask Me Anything with Taylor's Mereka Makerspace: Gearing up for Tomorrow - Shaping Community Through Education
agendas.description: Rashvin is the group CEO and Co-founder of Biji-biji Initiative and Me.Reka Education. His main areas of passion and experience lie in the fields of Sustainable Development, Youth Development, Social Entrepreneurship, and unlocking global financial and technological resources to achieve collective impact. Rashvin invites you to take part in an intimate conversation on how education and community empowerment can be used as important tools to forge a generation of underserved youth with an entrepreneurial and technical skills to bring themselves and their communities out of poverty.

agendas.title: Do-It-Yourself @ Home: Build Your Remote Control Car
agendas.description: Want to build your very own wooden remote control car? Join this workshop to learn the fundamentals of mechanical engineering and electronics in assembling this zippy car. You'll also get hands-on experience in using a laser cutter to make the body of the car.

agendas.title: Battle of the Music with Taylor's Symphony Orchestra x Taylor's L.I.V.E.Club x Taylor's Music Club
agendas.description: Music may be one of the things they have in common but nothing like a battle of three different musical clubs to bring out the winning spirit among them! Who will win the battle? Find out in our IG Live.

agendas.title: Guess Our Club with Mystery Club 1 & Mystery Club 2
agendas.description: Nothing like some mystery to heat things up with these two clubs and you. Yes that's right, all you have to do is guess which club they belong to. Sounds easy? Think again. Participate in our IG Live to be part of the fun!

agendas.title: BizPod Workshop: Journey of E-commerce: Zero To Hero​
agendas.description:
Join this session with Quan Ming to learn:

  1. How Quan Ming started
  2. What’s important in a store setup
  3. What are some of the tips and tricks used
  4. How to achieve 6 figures in a month/grow sales

agendas.title: Microcreds Tasters: Be a Future Ready Accountant
agendas.description: Join this class to understand about the evolution of responsibilities for accountancy professionals and learn about the mindset to fit into the future needs and business environment.

agendas.title: Ask Me Anything with Taylor's Mereka Makerspace: Gearing up for Tomorrow - Democracy and Liberty
agendas.description: Tricia Yeoh has been involved in public policy and policy making processes through her work with a cross section of individuals with in civil society, public official, political representatives, the media and private sector. She has contributed in making reforms within state and federal level policies within areas of anti-corruption, governance, accountability and economic freedom. Tricia invites you to take part in this close door conversation on what it takes for the youth of today to charge of the future of the country, be it through political engagement, awareness of our legal rights and etc.

agendas.title: Do-It-Yourself @ Home: Build Your Bluetooth Speaker
agendas.description: Ever wonder how you can build a wooden bluetooth speaker? Through this workshop, learn the fundamentals of fabrication and electronics in assembling your own speakers to play tunes at home. Also, you'll get hands-on experience in using a laser cutter to make the body of the speakers with your own customised details.

agendas.title: Name 3 in 3 seconds with KPMG Taylor's Ambassadors x Taylor's SHINE Ambassadors
agendas.description: Things we love in 3 seconds? Let's go - Taylorians, Campus and Taylor's Duckie! Phew that was easy. It may not be as easy for these 2 clubs as they battle out to name 3 in 3 seconds! Tune in to our IG Live to find out.

agendas.title: Who Did It Best with Taylor's Lakeside Dance Club and Taylor's SPART
agendas.description: Two clubs, 1 challenge each! Who can do it best? You decide! Tune in to our IG Live to find out!

agendas.title: Microcred Tasters: Data Visualisation in Data Science
agendas.description: Get a preview of various data types and relevant management tools, as you analyse and describe how popular big data platforms and analytical tools can be used for big data management. Learn techniques to handle data, discover meaningful information from unexploited data sources, and explore new data sources.

agendas.title: BizPod Workshop: Zero to Multimillion Revenue
agendas.description: Take the first step towards starting your side hustle in e-commerce! This workshop takes you through getting started on Shopee, strategic product listing, marketing, running paid ads, measuring performance, and becoming an expert seller.

allAgendas.title: Microcreds Tasters: Be a Future Ready Accountant
allAgendas.description: Join this class to understand about the evolution of responsibilities for accountancy professionals and learn about the mindset to fit into the future needs and business environment.

allAgendas.description: Two clubs, 1 challenge each! Who can do it best? You decide! Tune in to our IG Live to find out!

agendas.title: Ask Me Anything with Taylor's Mereka Makerspace: Gearing up for Tomorrow - Innovation in Civil Spaces
agendas.description: Pang Khee Teik is an activist, curator, organiser, photographer, editor and writer based in Malaysia. In 2008, while working as Programme Director at The Annexe Gallery, he co-founded the sexuality rights festival Seksualiti Merdeka in Kuala Lumpur together with fellow activists and artists. He is also the co-founder of QueerLapis.com, a website by and for LGBT people in Malaysia. Pang is presently the Programme Manager for Innovation For Change-East Asia. Pang Khee Teik invites you to an intimate conversation on how the youth of today can create their own spaces to forge changes that they want to see in their families, communities and country.

agendas.title: Do-It-Yourself @ Home: Build Your WiFi RGB LED Lamp
agendas.description: Wondering how you can build your own WiFi RGB LED Lamp? Join this interesting workshop to learn how to make your own WiFi-controlled lamp, while learning the basics of electronic assembly and app development. Also, get hands-on experience in using a laser cutter to make the body of the lamp with your own customised details.

agendas.title: Let's Get Real with Taylor's Girl Up x Taylor's Connect x VOCAL
agendas.description: Shedding light on important conversations no one talks about but should, join these clubs as they get real with each other and you on things that really matters! Tune in to our IG live to join the conversations.

agendas.title: There's an Imposter Among Us with Taylor's C.S.I. Volunteers x Taylor's Animal Wellfare Society x Taylor's Nature Club
agendas.description: All they have is 1 clue. Will they find the imposter among them? Tune in to our IG Live to find out!

agendas.title: Microcred Tasters: Supervised Machine Learning
agendas.description: Join the taster session of this Microcred that covers the basics of data visualisation and exploratory data analysis. Get an introduction to various data visualisation packages used for statistical analysis and discover the relationship between data analytics and data visualisation.

agendas.title: BizPod Workshop: What is Cryptocurrency and How to Create Your Own Coin in 5 mins
agendas.description:
Join this session with Nicholas Chin to learn:

  1. Bitcoin: What it is and why it is disrupting the traditional finance sector
  2. How traditional financial industry giants are utilising cryptocurrency & blockchain openly
  3. Usage of cryptocurrency in real life
  4. Expansion of crypto into digital utilities such as property, arts, gaming, payment & banking
  5. Creating your own cryptocurrency

agendas.title: TechStars Start-up Weekend​
agendas.description: An action-packed 54-hour event designed to provide superior experiential education for technical and non-technical entrepreneurs. Beginning with Friday night pitches, the event continues through brainstorming, business plan development, and basic prototype creation, culminating in Sunday night demos and presentations.

agendas.title: Microcred Tasters: Risk Communication and Management
agendas.description: The course expects students to gain practical updated knowledge that are supported by views of industry experts to set them on a path to be an independent and capable thinkers to resolve risk for organisations.

agendas.title: Microcred Tasters: UX Starter Kit
agendas.description: This course introduces the fundamentals and processes of UI/UX designs in creating personas, user flows and mental models. Learners will learn the building blocks of the entire UX design process from conceptualisation, prototyping to user testing and familiarise with the tools required in the building process. Learners will also navigate the ins and outs of information architecture by building wireframes and prototypes, and testing the designs with real audiences.

agendas.title: Pictionary with Taylor's Lakeside Pre-Medical Club x Wine & Dine
agendas.description: Two very different clubs are given a pen and a paper to draw and guess things both clubs aren't familiar with. Let's hope both clubs are familiar with how the human heart and a plate of creme brule looks like in drawing! Tune in to our IG Live to find out!

agendas.title: Battle of the Clubs with Taylor's Tradisi and Taylor's Futsal Club
agendas.description: When futsal challenges meets dance challenges, you already know things are about to get entertaining! Tune in to our IG Live to catch the action!

agendas.title: Taylor’s Chinese Society Virtual Charity Run 2021
agendas.description: Taylor’s Chinese Society Virtual Charity Run 2021 All About Love is dedicated to the perfect combination of sports and charity, bringing warmth and care to the community. In this online virtual charity run event, we will donate all the net profit of the event to USJ One Charity Home.

allAgendas.title: Microcred Tasters: Risk Communication and Management
allAgendas.description: The course expects students to gain practical updated knowledge that are supported by views of industry experts to set them on a path to be an independent and capable thinkers to resolve risk for organisations.

allAgendas.title: Pictionary with Taylor's Lakeside Pre-Medical Club x Wine & Dine
allAgendas.description: Two very different clubs are given a pen and a paper to draw and guess things both clubs aren't familiar with. Let's hope both clubs are familiar with how the human heart and a plate of creme brule looks like in drawing! Tune in to our IG Live to find out!

allAgendas.title: Taylor’s Chinese Society Virtual Charity Run 2021
allAgendas.description: Taylor’s Chinese Society Virtual Charity Run 2021 All About Love is dedicated to the perfect combination of sports and charity, bringing warmth and care to the community. In this online virtual charity run event, we will donate all the net profit of the event to USJ One Charity Home.

allAgendas.title: Microcred Tasters: UX Starter Kit
allAgendas.description: This course introduces the fundamentals and processes of UI/UX designs in creating personas, user flows and mental models. Learners will learn the building blocks of the entire UX design process from conceptualisation, prototyping to user testing and familiarise with the tools required in the building process. Learners will also navigate the ins and outs of information architecture by building wireframes and prototypes, and testing the designs with real audiences.

allAgendas.title: Battle of the Clubs with Taylor's Tradisi and Taylor's Futsal Club
allAgendas.description: When futsal challenges meets dance challenges, you already know things are about to get entertaining! Tune in to our IG Live to catch the action!

agendas.title: Microcred Tasters: 360° Video Telling
agendas.description: Introduces the immersive, unique and authentic perspective of 360° video storytelling, and its experimental features. The full Microcred demonstrates the 360° video story content development work flow, and the potential of 360° videos in the Virtual Reality (VR) spectrum.

agendas.title: Microcred Tasters: Competing with Big Data Analytics
agendas.description: With information widespread in various platforms and sources, big data is crucially prevalent to business organisations in a competitive environment, especially in Industry Revolution 4.0 (IR4.0). In this session, get an introduction to the various techniques and best practices of big data analytics. You can learn to identify and see how data is managed from various sources and platforms that can help companies make important decisions.

allAgendas.title: Microcred Tasters: 360° Video Telling
allAgendas.description: Introduces the immersive, unique and authentic perspective of 360° video storytelling, and its experimental features. The full Microcred demonstrates the 360° video story content development work flow, and the potential of 360° videos in the Virtual Reality (VR) spectrum.

allAgendas.title: Microcred Tasters: Competing with Big Data Analytics
allAgendas.description: With information widespread in various platforms and sources, big data is crucially prevalent to business organisations in a competitive environment, especially in Industry Revolution 4.0 (IR4.0). In this session, get an introduction to the various techniques and best practices of big data analytics. You can learn to identify and see how data is managed from various sources and platforms that can help companies make important decisions.

speakers.name: Datuk Nicol David
speakers.details:
A true legend of the game, Nicol David is one of the most decorated sports stars of a generation and has broken a succession of remarkable records during her career to date - capturing the hearts of thousands of adoring fans in the process.

The Penang-born superstar burst onto the scene in 1999 when she won her first Women's World Junior Squash Championship and by January 2006, she became the first Asian woman ever to reach the World No.1 spot.

The 8 time World Champion is an Ambassador for the Laureus Sport for Good Foundation and a United Nations Development Programme Goodwill Ambassador for Malaysia.

David believes that the youth can be a positive force for development, peace, and democracy. She has spoken out on the importance of values of understanding, solidarity, respect and communication across all cultures.

speakingEvents.topic: TU Exploration Day: Keynote Interview with Datuk Nicol David

speakers.name: Yuna
speakers.details:
In a short period of time, Yuna has risen from regional D.I.Y. notoriety to international stardom recording alongside Pharrell Williams, touring with Lollapalooza and pairing with hitmaker Usher.

The charismatic young singer-songwriter is also the first Malaysian artist to conquer the American market.

Her personally charged songs are deeply felt yet melodically irresistible, combining her engaging voice and expressive songcraft with imaginative production to create distinctive music that's won her comparisons with the likes of Feist, Adele and Norah Jones.

speakingEvents.topic: TC Exploration Day: Keynote Interview with Yuna

speakers.name: Datin Paduka Marina Mahathir
speakers.details:
Marina Mahathir is a writer and activist. She has been a newspaper columnist for over 25 years and led an HIV/AIDS NGO for 12 years. In 2009 she joined Sisters in Islam and served on the board for six years.

She is currently the chair of the board of Musawah, the global movement for justice and equality in the Muslim family. Marina has also produced TV programmes, feature films and a documentary and founded Zafigo.com, a website for women and travel.

She also hosted ZafigoX, an annual event on women and travel and during the Covid pandemic in 2020, a fortnightly online chat show called Hello Zafigo. In 2021 she began hosting a podcast on books called Busy Reading Books by Zafigo. She is currently finishing a memoir to be published in November 2021 by Penguin Random House.

speakingEvents.topic: TU Exploration Day: Keynote Interview with Datin Paduka Marina Mahathir

speakers.name: Jonathan Lee
speakers.details: With over 20 years of commercial experience. The last 10 years alone he has held several senior roles from helping put Malaysia on the startup map globally. To starting his own healthcare startup, to heading business development for one Malaysia largest public listed tech companies. To building and fundraising for an early-stage VC firm. He holds a masters in Entrepreneurship and a bachelor of commerce from Otago School of Business.

speakingEvents.topic: BizPod Workshop: Do this Before Start-up

speakers.name: Allen Sanchez
speakers.details:
I enjoy helping new ventures and ideas come to life. Over the last 5 years I’ve coached nearly 30 teams to build their minimum viable product and acquire their first users.

Yet, I don’t believe that only “tech people” have the skills needed to bring a tech product to life. With so many new tools out there, I believe that almost anyone can prototype their product idea very quickly and cheaply...and without having to write any code!

In addition to coaching other teams, I’ve also been a part of 3 EdTech ventures myself: one that failed, one that I’m no longer a part of, and one that I sold in 2020. I’m happy to share my experiences and skills with others, so they can avoid many of the mistakes I made along the way!

speakingEvents.topic: BizPod Workshop: Build a Tech prototype in 24 hours

speakers.name: Nianci Phang
speakers.position: Regional Marketing and Business Development, Alibaba Group
speakers.details:
Nianci is a marketing professional with experience in integrated marketing strategy, marketing communications and public relations, and Branding. She is a journalism and media graduate from Swinburne University of Technology in Melbourne, Australia and has extensive experience in a wide range of companies in marketing roles in companies from Astro, iProperty Group, to Lazada.

She is currently the Country Marketing Lead for Alibaba.com Malaysia. Prior to her current position in Alibaba Group, she previously served in MNCs, in broadcasting / media, technology and e-commerce organisations, Lazada Malaysia, iProperty.com.my and Astro Malaysia.

As a seasoned marketer, she is experienced in crafting O2O (online to offline) brand building strategies that results in growth for business goals. She is also a Google Cerfitied Adwords and Analytics individual, and has been advising businesses and SMEs on internet and e-commerce strategies. She is also a regular invited speaker at seminars and conferences.

speakingEvents.topic: BizPod Workshop: The Business of E-Commerce

speakers.name: Tan Quan Ming
speakers.details:
Having worked in Global MNC’s Forbes 500 (Royal Dutch Shell, British American Tobacco) with multi-talented and multi-cultural individuals, Quan Ming is a balanced blend of applying ASIAN and WESTERN style of work caliber depending on situational needs. He can be firm and/or soft depending on the audience and this gives him an unfair advantage over his peers. Always a team player and understands that working well with people is among the most important skill to have besides being able to solve complex problems, being analytical,​ ​innovative​ ​and​ ​creative. He was also awarded Shell Special Recognition Award (2013).​

​Currently, co-founder of 2 E-Commerce companies (listed above), he has been featured multiple times on Lazada Masterclass, national TV: TV3, as well as Alibaba.com.

speakingEvents.topic: Journey of E-commerce: Zero To Hero​

speakers.name: Jamie Kay
speakers.details:
Key Account Manager, STX Direct
2 years experience in E-commerce Industry
Bachelor of Business, Victoria University
Ex Corporate Banker

Selling journey on Shopee:
March 2020
Monthly Rev <RM30k
<100 Followers

Current:
Monthly Rev ~RM2.1M
143K Followers

speakingEvents.topic: BizPod Workshop: Zero to Multimillionaire

speakers.name: Nicholas Chin
speakers.details:
Nic Chin is an entrepreneurial and driven business executive, with outstanding success in software ventures, product development, business growth and cross-functional leadership for both start up and established high-tech companies. Over the years, he has gained solid backgrounds in financial trading technology, product marketing, operations and management. Through creativity and dogged determination, he has effectively led and achieved remarkable results, earning several recognitions and a high reputation for consistency.

Nic's obsession for Crypto Currencies, Blockchain Technology and FinTech lead him to co-found Bitto Exchange in 2017. (This platform provides an ultimate venue where crypto currency traders and investors could find each other and create a transparent, safe and efficient investment environment). BITTO also recently collaborated with YFSwap for the deployment of a DeFi version of Bitcoin called YFBTC, which is a combination of two successful Crypto, YFI and BTC. And BITTO which is led by Nic and his team is also looking into creating an NFT platform with BITTO partners.

speakingEvents.topic: BizPod Workshop: What is cryptocurrency and how to create your own coin in 5mins?

speakers.name: Mr. Pyi Phyo Aung
speakers.details:
Born in Mandalay, Myanmar, Pyi Phyo Aung has been passionate about electronics since childhood.

After graduating from UTM, he worked in Naypyidaw Engineering Center (NEC), Mandalay as a lecturer, teaching practical lab sections to engineering diploma students. He has supervised/co-supervised and personally built microcontroller and IoT Projects with university students and hobbyists.

Other than being a distinguished trainer, he also wrote and published a technical book about Arduino in Burmese, which became a best-selling technical book in 2018.

speakingEvents.topic: Taylor's Mereka Makerspace Workshop: Build Your Remote Control Car
Taylor's Mereka Makerspace Workshop: Build Your Bluetooth Speaker
Taylor's Mereka Makerspace Workshop: Build Your WiFi RGB LED Lamp

speakers.name: Janaronson Nagarajah
speakers.details: With more than 15 years in youth development, he presently leads platforms and opportunities designed to enhance students’ life skills and emotional well-being holistically. He is the pioneering team leader of the Life Skills Programme which takes all First-Year undergraduates through a transformative experiential learning journey, focusing on self-awareness, self-management, social awareness and relationship management. The initiative was recognized in the national level, winning the Malaysian Education Minister's Special Award for Curriculum Design and Innovative Delivery, under the category of Immersive Face to Face Learning in 2018. Ronson lives with his motto of “inspiring and helping young people to be productive life-long learners”.

speakingEvents.topic: College: Go Far, Go Together

speakers.name: Sheila Yvonne Jayasainan
speakers.details: Sheila Yvonne Jayasainan teaches Public Relations, Research Methods and Media Literacy at the School of Pre-University Studies, Taylor’s College, Malaysia. She has over 15-years of experience in academia and 10-years in the field of public relations. She also worked as a corporate trainer specializing in communication skills, presentation skills and professional writing. Her research interest is in new media, public relations and education technology.

speakingEvents.topic: College: Go Far, Go Together

speakers.name: Dr Elangkovan A/L Narayanan Alagas
speakers.details:
Dr. Elangkovan is a well-established Senior lecturer and trainer in Management, Strategic Management and Organisational Behaviour, with 25 years of experience.

His experience in corporate & hospitality management training & consultancy includes Putrajaya Marriott Hotel, The Saujana Kuala Lumpur, Villa Resorts Maldives, Hyatt Hotel Sabah, Affin Bank, Wasco Engineers, IJM, MSIG, Arab Malaysian Bank, Astro & Royal Selangor.

He is currently involved in the field of Human Resources Management, specialising in organisational learning, leadership & education relating to staff behaviour & mobility.

speakingEvents.topic: Microcred Tasters: Competitive Strategic Analysis

speakers.name: Ms Marzura Binti Abdul Malek
speakers.details:
Ms Marzura Binti Abdul Malek is a senior lecturer in Taylor’s University, Subang Jaya, Selangor. Malaysia. She specialises in areas of Public Relations and has more than 15 years of industrial experience in this field. She is currently, pursuing her PhD studies and her research area includes content analysis of radio programmes in Malaysia. Her other research interest areas are community service for youth, innovations in business and children and media.

She is a member of the Institute of Public Relations Malaysia (IPRM) and a lifetime member of the Malaysian Society for Higher Education Policy and Research Development, and an associate member of Public Relations and Communication Association (PRCA)

speakingEvents.topic: Microcred Tasters: Risk Communication and Management

speakers.name: Dr Feranita
speakers.details:
Dr. Feranita is a Senior Lecturer at Taylor’s Business School, and the founder and leader of Family Enterprise & Entrepreneurship Hub at Taylor's University.

She received her PhD in management from Lancaster University, UK. Her research centres around family business, entrepreneurship, innovation & university-industry collaboration. She has presented her research at international conferences & published in journals.

Having over 10 years of experience of dealing with business families, she's also a certified professional coach, training startups, business families, and entrepreneurs.

speakingEvents.topic: Microcred Tasters: Dynamic Pitching For StartUps

speakers.name: Mr Murugan A/L Krisnamoorthy
speakers.details:
Well-established Senior Lecturer and Trainer in the field of Supply Chain, Logistics Management, Entrepreneurship & Food Media, with over 15 years of industry experience in Supply Chain & Logistics Management at a Senior Manager Position.

Other than amassing practical experience in various improvement exercises for Supply Chain-based operations, he's been involved in teaching & training for the past 9 years.

In the academic field, he's trained many students as food business entrepreneurs and presented papers in local & international conferences.

speakingEvents.topic: Microcred Tasters: Menu That Brings Profit: Menu Design & Development

speakers.name: Ong M. G.
speakers.position: Chief Academy Officer of 6Biz Sdn Bhd
speakers.details:
M.G. is a best-selling author on Amazon.com with a #1 hit in four different categories within Amazon America and Australia.

He has over 30 years of experience in digital consultancy, senior management, project management and training, with multinationals such as KPMG and NASDAQ-listed ININ, serving clients in property, banking, insurance and other high-value industries across Asia Pacific & Japan, Oceania, Europe and North America.

He's also an advocate of business digital transformation focusing on big data analytics and robotic process automation.

speakingEvents.topic: Microcred Tasters: Data Analytics and the Keys to Business Transformation

speakers.name: Boonsiri Somchit
speakers.details:
Author of bestselling leadership book, 'When the Chicken Dies, Everyone Cries', Boon has had an illustrious career in the high-tech semiconductor industry as the CEO & Corporate VP of Advanced Micro Devices Global Services.

Boon & her team built the shared service from scratch to support Asia but their 'can do, never give up' attitude led to them being tasked with expanding to the world. After 19 years in AMD, she decided to follow her passion for building up leaders.

speakingEvents.topic: Microcred Tasters: The Rise of Global Business Services (GBS) Qualification

speakers.name: Pang Khee Teik
speakers.position: Programme Manager, Innovation For Change - East Asia
speakers.details:
Activist, curator, organiser, photographer, editor and writer based in Malaysia. In 2008, he co-founded the sexuality rights festival Seksualiti Merdeka, which was banned by the police in 2011.

Two years later, Pang completed an MA in Gender, Sexuality & Culture at Birkbeck College, under the Chevening Scholarship. Returning to KL, he organised arts and activism bazaar Art For Grabs, where he was arrested under sedition charges in 2016.

He continued organising workshops while starting up QueerLapis.com, a website by and for LGBT people in Malaysia.

speakingEvents.topic: Ask Me Anything with Taylor's Mereka Makerspace: Gearing up for tomorrow - Innovation in Civil Spaces
